source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXXVI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No greater gift could God have given to men than in making His Word, by which He created all things, their Head, and joining them to Him as His members: that the Son of God might become also the Son of man, one God with the Father, one Man with men; so that when we speak to God in prayer for mercy, we do not separate the Son from Him; and when the Body of the Son prays, it separates not its Head from itself: and it is one Saviour of His Body, our Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of God, who both prays for us, and prays in us, and is prayed to by us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He prays for us, as our Priest; He prays in us, as our Head; He is prayed to by us, as our God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore recognise in Him our words, and His words in us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor when anything is said of our Lord Jesus Christ, especially in prophecy, implying a degree of humility below the dignity of God, let us hesitate to ascribe it to Him who did not hesitate to join Himself unto us.…He is prayed to in the form of God, in the form of a servant He prayeth; there the Creator, here created; assuming unchanged the creature, that it might be changed, and making us with Himself one Man, Head and Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore we pray to Him, through Him, in Him; and we speak with Him, and He speaks with us; we speak in Him, He speaks in us the prayer of this Psalm, which is entitled, “A Prayer of David.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For our Lord was, according to the flesh, the son of David; but according to His divine nature, the Lord of David, and his Maker.…Let no one then, when he hears these words, say, Christ speaketh not; nor again say, I speak not; nay rather, if he own himself to be in the Body of Christ, let him say both, Christ speaks, and I speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be thou unwilling to say anything without Him, and He saith nothing without thee.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bow down Thine ear, O Lord, and hear me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He speaks in the form of a servant: speak thou, O servant, in the form of thy Lord: “Bow down Thine ear, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He bows down His ear, if thou dost not lift up thy neck: for unto the humble He draweth near: from him that is exalted He removes afar off, except whom He Himself hath exalted from being humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God then bows down His ear unto us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is above, we below: He in a high place, we in a lowly one, yet not deserted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For scarcely for a just man will one die: yet for a good man peradventure one would even dare to die:” but our Lord died for the wicked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no merits of ours had gone before, for which the Son of God should die: but the more, because there were no merits, was His mercy great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How sure then, how firm is the promise, by which for the righteous He keepeth His life, who for the wicked gave His own death!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I am poor and in misery.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the rich then He boweth not down His ear: unto the poor and him that is in misery He boweth down His ear, that is, unto the humble, and him that confesseth, unto him that is in need of mercy: not unto him that is full, who lifteth up himself and boasteth, as if he wanted nothing, and saith, “I thank Thee that I am not as this Publican.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the rich Pharisee boasted of his merits: the poor Publican confessed his sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet do not take what I have said, my brethren, in such a way, as if God does not hear those who have gold and silver, and a household, and farms, if they happen to be born to this estate, or hold such a rank in the world: only let them remember the Apostle’s words: “Charge those who are rich in this world, that they be not highminded.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those that are not highminded are poor in God, and to the poor and needy and those in want He inclines His ear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they know that their hope is not in gold and silver, nor in those things in which for a time they seem to abound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is enough that riches ruin them not; it is enough that they do them no harm: for good they can do them none.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What certainly profiteth is a work of mercy, done by a rich or by a poor man: by a rich man, with will and deed; by a poor man, with will alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore he is such an one as despiseth in himself everything which is wont to swell men with pride, he is one of God’s poor: He inclines unto him His ear, for He knows that his heart is contrite.…Was it really for the merit of his poverty that the poor man was carried away by Angels, or was it for the sin of his riches that the rich man was sent away to be tormented?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that poor man is signified the honour which is paid to humility, in that rich man the condemnation which awaits pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will prove shortly that it was not riches but pride which was tormented in that rich man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is certain that the poor man was carried into the bosom of Abraham: of Abraham himself Scripture saith that he had here very much gold and silver, and was rich on the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If every one that is rich is hurried away to be tormented, how could Abraham have gone before that poor man, so as to be ready to receive him when carried to his bosom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Abraham in his riches was poor, humble, reverencing all commands, and obeying them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So true was it that he counted all those riches for nothing, that on God’s command he was ready to sacrifice his son, for whom he was keeping his riches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Learn therefore ye to be poor and needy, whether ye have anything in this world, or whether ye have not.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Preserve Thou My Soul, for I am holy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I know not whether any one could say this, “I am holy,” but He who was in the world without sin: He by whom all sins were not committed but remitted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We own it to be His voice saying, “Preserve Thou My Soul, for I am holy;” of course in that form of a servant which He had assumed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in that was flesh, in that, was also a Soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He was not, as some have said, only Flesh and the Word: but Flesh and Soul also, and the Word, and all this, One Son of God, One Christ, One Saviour; in the form of God equal to the Father, in the form of a servant the Head of the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore I hear, “for I am holy,” I recognise His voice: yet do I exclude my own?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely He speaks inseparably from His body when He speaks thus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall I then dare to say, “For I am holy”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If holy as making holy, and as needing none to sanctify, I should be proud and false: but if holy as made holy, as it is written, “Be ye holy, for I am holy,” then the body of Christ may venture, and that one Man “crying from the end of the earth,” may venture with his Head, and under his Head, to say, “For I am holy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he hath received the grace of holiness, the grace of Baptism, and of remission of sins.…Say unto thy God, I am holy, for Thou hast sanctified me: because I received, not because I had: because Thou gavest, not because I deserved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For on another side thou art beginning to do an injury to our Lord Jesus Christ Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if all Christians who are faithful and have been baptized in Him have put Him on, as the Apostle saith, “As many as are baptized in Christ have put on Christ:” if they have been made members of His body, and say that they are not holy, they do injury to their Head, of whom they are members, and yet not holy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look thou where thou art and from thy Head assume dignity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou wert in darkness, “but now light in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Ye were sometime darkness,” he saith: but did ye remain darkness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it for this the Enlightener came, that ye might still remain darkness, or that in Him ye might become light?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, every Christian by himself, therefore also the whole body of Christ, may say, it may cry everywhere, while it suffers tribulations, various temptations and offences, it may say, “Preserve Thou my soul, for I am holy: my God, save Thy servant, that putteth his trust in Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See thou, that holy man is not proud, since he putteth his trust in God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be merciful unto me, O Lord, for I have cried unto Thee all day” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not “one day:” understand “all day” to mean continually: from the time that the body of Christ groans being in afflictions, until the end of the world, when afflictions pass away, that man groaneth and calleth upon God: and each one of us after his measure hath his part in that cry in the whole body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast cried in thy days, and thy days have passed away: another hath come after thee, and cried in his days: and thou here, he there, another elsewhere: the body of Christ crieth all the day, its members departing and succeeding one another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One Man it is that reaches to the end of the world: the same members of Christ cry, and some members already rest in Him, some still cry, some when we shall be at rest will cry, and after them others will cry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the whole body of Christ whose voice He hears, saying, “Unto Thee have I cried all the day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Head on the right hand of the Father intercedes for us: some members He recovereth, others He scourgeth, others He cleanseth, others He comforteth, others He is creating, others calling, others recalling, others correcting, others restoring.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Make glad the soul of Thy servant: for unto Thee, O Lord, have I lifted up my soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Make it glad, for unto Thee have I lifted it up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was on earth, and from the earth it felt bitterness: lest it should wither away in bitterness, lest it should lose all the sweetness of Thy grace, I lifted it up unto Thee: make Thou it glad with Thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou alone art gladness: the whole world is full of bitterness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely with reason He admonishes His members to lift up their hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May they hear and do it: may they lift up unto Him what on earth is ill.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There the heart decayeth not, if it be lifted up to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou hadst corn in thy rooms below, thou wouldest take it up higher, lest it should grow rotten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest thou remove thy corn, and dost thou suffer thy heart to rot on the earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wouldest take thy corn up higher: lift up thy heart to heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how can I, dost thou say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What ropes are needed? what machines? what ladders?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy affections are the steps: thy will the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By loving thou mountest, by neglect thou descendest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Standing on the earth thou art in heaven, if thou lovest God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the heart is not so raised as the body is raised: the body to be lifted up changes its place: the heart to be lifted up changes its will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou, Lord, art good and gracious” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5).…Even prayers are often hindered by vain thoughts, so that the heart scarcely remains fixed on God: and it would hold itself so as to be fixed, and somehow flees from itself, and finds no frames in which it can enclose itself, no bars by which it may keep in its flights and wandering movements, and stand still to be made glad by its God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Scarcely does one such prayer occur amongst many.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Each one might say that this happened to him, but that it happened not to others, if we did not find in the holy Scripture David praying in a certain place, and saying, “Since I have found my heart, O Lord, so that I might pray unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said that he had found his heart, as if it were wont to flee from him, and he to follow it like a fugitive, and not be able to catch it, and to cry to God, “For my heart hath deserted me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, my brethren, thinking over what he saith here, I think I see what he meaneth by “gracious.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I seem to feel that for this reason he calls God gracious, because He bears with those failings of ours, and yet expects prayer from us, in order to make us perfect: and when we have given it to Him, He receives it gratefully, and listens to it, and remembers not those many prayers which we pour out unthinkingly, and accepts the one which we can scarcely find.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what man is there, my brethren, who, on being addressed by his friend, when he wishes to answer his address, sees his friend turn away from him and speak to another, who is there who would bear this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or if you appeal to a judge, and set him up to hear you, and all at once, while you are speaking to him, pass from him, and begin to converse with your friend, who would endure this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet God endures the hearts of so many persons who pray and think of different things.…What then?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Must we despair of mankind, and say that every man is already condemned into whose prayers any wandering thoughts have crept and interrupted them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we say this, my brethren, I know not what hope remains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore because there is some hope before God, because His mercy is great, let us say unto Him, “For unto Thee, O Lord, have I lifted up my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how have I lifted it up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As I could, as Thou gavest me strength, as I could catch it when it fled away.…From infirmity I sink: heal Thou me, and I shall stand: strengthen Thou me, and I shall be strong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But until Thou do this, Thou bearest with me: “For Thou, Lord, art good and gracious, and of great mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, not only “of mercy,” but “of great mercy:” for as our iniquity abounds, so also aboundeth Thy mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Unto all that call upon Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it then which Scripture saith in many places: “They shall call, and I will not hear them”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet surely Thou art merciful to all that call upon Thee; but that some call, yet call not upon Him, of whom it is said, “They have not called upon God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They call, but not on God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou callest upon whatever thou lovest: thou callest upon whatever thou callest unto thyself, whatever thou wishest to come unto thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore if thou callest upon God for this reason, in order that money may come unto thee, that an inheritance may come unto thee, that worldly rank may come unto thee, thou callest upon those things which thou desirest may come unto thee: but thou makest God the helper of thy desires, not the listener to thy needs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God is good, if He gives what thou wishest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if thou wishest ill, will He not then be more merciful by not giving?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then, if He gives not, then is God nothing to thee; and thou sayest, How much I have prayed, how often I have prayed, and have not been heard!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why, what didst thou ask?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps that thy enemy might die.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if he at the same time were praying for thy death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who created thee, created him also: thou art a man, he too is a man; but God is the Judge: He hears both, and He grants their prayer to neither.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art sad, because thou wast not heard when praying against him; be glad, because his prayer was not heard against thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou sayest, I did not ask for this; I asked not for the death of my enemy, but for the life of my child; what ill did I ask?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou askedst no ill, as thou didst think.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if “he was taken away, lest wickedness should alter his understanding.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he was a sinner, thou sayest, and therefore I wished him to live, that he might be corrected.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wishedst him to live, that he might become better; what if God knew, that if he lived he would become worse?…If, therefore, thou callest on God as God, be confident thou shalt be heard: thou hast part in that verse: “And of great mercy unto all that call upon Thee.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think, brethren, and reflect what good things God giveth unto sinners: and learn hence what He keepeth for His own servants.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To sinners who blaspheme Him every day He giveth the sky and the earth, He giveth springs, fruits, health, children, wealth, abundance: all these good things none giveth but God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who giveth such things to sinners, what thinkest thou He keeps for His faithful ones?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is this to be believed of Him, that He who giveth such things to the bad, keepeth nothing for the good?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay verily He doth keep, not earth, but heaven for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Too common a thing perhaps I say when I say heaven; Himself rather, who made the heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fair is heaven, but fairer is the Maker of heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I see the heavens, Him I see not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because thou hast eyes to see the heavens: a heart thou hast not yet to see the Maker of heaven: therefore came He from heaven to earth, to cleanse the heart, that He may be seen who made heaven and earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wait thou with full patience for salvation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By what treatment to cure thee, He knoweth: by what cutting, what burning, He knoweth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast brought sickness on thyself by sinning: He comes not only to nurse, but also to cut and to burn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seest thou not how much men suffer under the hands of physicians, when a man promises them an uncertain hope?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt be cured, says the physician: thou wilt be cured, if I cut.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a man who speaks, and to a man that he speaks: neither is he sure who speaks, nor he who hears, for he who is speaking to the man hath not made man, and knows not perfectly what is passing in man: yet at the words of a man who knows not what is passing in man, man sooner believeth, submits his limbs, suffers himself to be bound, often without being bound is cut or burned; and receives perhaps health for a few days, even when just healed not knowing when he may die: perhaps, while being healed, dies; perhaps cannot be healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to whom hath God promised anything, and deceived him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fix my prayer in Thy ears, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great earnestness of him who prays!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, let not my prayer go out of Thine ears, fix it then in Thine ears.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How did he travail that he might fix his prayer in the ears of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let God answer and say to us; Wouldest thou that I fix thy prayer in My ears?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fix My law in thy heart; “and attend to the voice of my prayer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the day of my trouble I have cried unto Thee, for Thou hast heard me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A little before he had said, All the day have I cried, all the day have I been troubled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no Christian then say that there is any day in which he is not troubled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By “all the day” we have understood the whole of time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, is there trouble even when it is well with us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even so, trouble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is there trouble?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “as long as we are in the body we are absent from the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let what will abound here, we are not yet in that country whither we are hastening to return.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He to whom foreign travel is sweet, loveth not his country: if his country is sweet, travel is bitter; if travel is bitter, all the day there is trouble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When is there not trouble?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When there is joy in one’s country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “At Thy right hand are delights for evermore.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt fill me with joy,” he saith, “with Thy countenance: that I may see the delight of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There toil and groaning shall pass away: there shall be not prayer but praise; there Alleluia, there Amen, the voice in concord with Angels; there vision without failing and love without weariness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So long therefore as we are not there, ye see that we are not in that which is good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But do all things abound?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If all things abound, see if thou art assured that all things perish not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I have what I had not: more money is come to me which I had not before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps more fear too is come, which thou hadst not before: perhaps thou wast so much the more secure as thou wast the poorer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fine, be it that thou hast wealth, that thou hast redundance of this world’s affluence, that thou hast assurance given thee that all this shall not perish; besides this, that God say unto thee, Thou shalt remain for ever in these things, they shall be for ever with thee, but My face thou shalt not see.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none ask counsel of the flesh: ask ye counsel of the Spirit: let your heart answer you; let hope, faith, charity, which has begun to be in you, answer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then we were to receive assurance that we should always be in affluence of worldly goods, and if God were to say to us, My face ye shall not see, would ye rejoice in these goods?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some one might perhaps choose to rejoice, and say, These things abound unto me, it is well with me, I ask no more.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath not yet begun to be a lover of God: he hath not yet begun to sigh like one far from home.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far be it, far be it from us: let them retire, all those seductions: let them retire, those false blandishments: let them be gone, those words which they say daily unto us, “Where is thy God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us pour out our soul over us, let us confess in tears, let us groan in confession, let us sigh in misery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever is present with us besides our God, is not sweet: we would not have all things that He hath given, if He gives not Himself who gave all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Among the gods there is none like unto Thee, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What did he say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Among the gods,” etc. Let the Pagans make for themselves what gods they will; let them bring workmen in silver and in gold, furbishers, sculptors; let them make gods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What kind of gods?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Having eyes, and seeing not; and the other things which the Psalm mentions in what follows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we do not worship these, he says; we do not worship them, these are symbols.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then do ye worship?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Something else that is worse: for the gods of the gentiles are devils.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Neither, say they, do we worship devils.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have certainly nothing else in your temples, nothing else inspires your prophets than a devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what do ye say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We worship Angels, we have Angels as gods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know not altogether what Angels are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Angels worship the one God, and favour not men who wish to worship Angels and not God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we find Angels of high rank forbidding men to adore them, and commanding them to adore the true God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when they say Angels, suppose they mean men, since it is said, “I have said, Ye are Gods, and all the children of the Most Highest.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever man thinks to the contrary, that which was made is not like Him who made it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Except God, whatever else there is in the universe was made by God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What a difference there is between Him who made, and that which was made, who can worthily imagine?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore this man said, “there is none like unto Thee, O Lord: there is not one that can do as thou doest.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how much God is unlike them he said not, because it cannot be said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let your Charity attend: God is ineffable: we more easily say what He is not than what He is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou thinkest of the earth; this is not God: thou thinkest of the sea; this is not God: of all things which are in the earth, men and animals; this is not God: of all things which are in the sea, which fly through the air; this is not God: whatever shines in the sky, the stars, sun and moon; this is not God: the heaven itself; this is not God: think of the Angels, Virtues, Powers, Archangels, Thrones, Seats, Principalities; this is not God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is He then?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I could only tell thee, what He is not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Askest thou what He is?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “the eye hath not seen, nor the ear heard, nor hath risen up into the heart of man.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All nations that Thou hast made shall come and worship before Thee, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has announced the Church: “All nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If there is any nation which God hath not made, it will not worship Him: but there is no nation which God hath not made; because God made Adam and Eve, the source of all nations, thence all nations sprang.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All nations therefore hath God made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When was this said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When before Him there worshipped none but a few holy men in one people of the Hebrews, then this was said: and see now what it is which was said: “All nations that Thou hast made,” etc. When these things were spoken, they were not seen, and they were believed: now that they are seen, why are they denied?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All nations that Thou hast made shall come and worship before Thee, O Lord, and shall glorify Thy Name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou art great, and doing wondrous things: Thou alone art the great God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man call himself great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some were to be who would call themselves great: against these it is said, “Thou alone art the great God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what great thing is ascribed to God, when it is said that He alone is the great God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who knows not that He is the great God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because there were to be some who would call themselves great and make God little, against these it is said, “Thou alone art the great God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what Thou sayest is fulfilled, not what those say who call themselves great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath God said by His Spirit?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What saith he, whoever he is, who calleth himself great?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Far from it: God is not worshipped in all nations: all nations have perished, Africa alone remains.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This thou sayest, who callest thyself great: another thing He saith who alone is the great God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What saith He, who alone is the great God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I see what the only great God hath said: let man be silent, who is falsely great; great only in appearance, because he disdains to be small.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who disdains to be small?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who saith this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever will be great among you, said the Lord, shall be your servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If that man had wished to be the servant of his brethren, he would not have separated them from their mother: but when he wishes to be great, and wishes not to be small, as would be for his welfare, God, who resisteth the proud, and giveth grace to the humble, because He alone is great, fulfilleth all things which He predicted, and contradicteth those who blaspheme.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For such persons blaspheme against Christ, who say that the Church has perished from the whole world, and is left only in Africa.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou wert to say to him, Thou wilt lose thy villa, he would perhaps scarcely keep from laying his hand upon thee: and yet he says, that Christ has lost His inheritance, redeemed by His own Blood!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See now what a wrong he does, my brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Scripture says, “In a wide nation is the king’s honour; but in the domination of the people is the affliction of a prince.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This wrong then thou dost unto Christ, to say that His people is diminished to that small number.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it for this thou wast born, for this thou callest thyself a Christian, that thou mayest grudge Christ His glory, whose sign thou sayest that thou bearest on thy forehead, and hast lost out of thy heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In a wide nation is the king’s honour: acknowledge thy King: give Him glory, give Him a wide nation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What wide nation shall I give Him, dost thou say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Choose not to give Him from thy own heart, and thou wilt give aright.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence am I to give? thou wilt say.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, give from hence: “All nations that Thou hast made shall come and worship before Thee, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say this, confess this, and thou hast given a wide nation: for all nations in One are one: this is very oneness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as there is a Church and Churches, and those are Churches which also are a Church, so that is a nation which was nations: formerly nations, many nations, now one nation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why one nation?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because one faith, one hope, one charity, one expectation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, why not one nation, if one country?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our country is heavenly, our country is Jerusalem: whoever is not a citizen of it, belongs not to that nation: but whoever is a citizen of it is in that one nation of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this nation, from the east to the west, from the north and the sea, is extended through the four quarters of the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This God saith: From the east and west, from the north and the sea, give glory to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This He foretold, this He fulfilled, who alone is great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him therefore who would not be little cease from saying this against Him who alone is great: for there cannot be two great, God and Donatus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lead me, O Lord, in Thy way, and I will walk in Thy truth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy way, Thy truth, Thy life, is Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore belongeth the Body to Him, and the Body is of Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am the Way, and the Truth, and the Life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lead me, O Lord, in Thy way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what way?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will walk in Thy truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is one thing to lead to the way, another to guide in the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold man everywhere poor, everywhere in need of help.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who are beside the way are not Christians, or not yet Catholics: let them be guided to the way: but when they have been brought to the way and made Catholics in Christ, they must be guided by Him in the way itself, lest they fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now assuredly they walk in the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lead me, O Lord, in Thy way:” surely I am now in Thy way, lead me there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will walk in Thy truth:” while Thou leadest I shall not err: if Thou let me go, I shall err.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pray then that He let thee not go, but lead thee even to the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth He lead thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By always admonishing, always giving thee His hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the arm of the Lord, to whom is it revealed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in giving His Christ He giveth His hand: in giving His hand, He giveth His Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He leadeth to the way, in leading to His Christ: He leadeth in the way, by leading in His Christ, and Christ is truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lead me,” therefore, “O Lord, in Thy way, and I will walk in Thy truth:” in Him verily who said, “I am the Way, and the Truth, and the Life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou who leadest in the way and the truth, whither leadest Thou, but unto life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Him then, unto Him Thou leadest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let my heart be made glad, so that it may fear Thy name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is then fear in gladness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How can there be gladness, if fear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not fear wont to be painful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There will hereafter be gladness without fear, now gladness with fear; for not yet is there perfect security, nor perfect gladness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If there is no gladness, we faint: if full security, we rejoice wrongly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore may He both sprinkle on us gladness, and strike fear into us, that by the sweetness of gladness He may lead us to the abode of security; by giving us fear, may cause us not to rejoice wrongly, and to withdraw from the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore saith the Psalm: “Serve the Lord in fear, and rejoice unto Him with trembling:” so also saith the Apostle Paul; “Work out your own salvation with fear and trembling; for it is God that worketh in you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever prosperity comes then, my brethren, is rather to be feared: those things which ye think to be prosperous, are rather temptations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An inheritance cometh, there cometh wealth, there is an abundant overflow of some happiness: these are temptations: take care that they corrupt you not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever prosperity also there is according to Christ, and the true love of Christ: if perhaps thou hast gained thy wife, who was of the party of Donatus: if thy sons have been made believers who were Pagans: if perhaps thou hast gained thy friend who wished to draw thee away to the theatres, and thou hast drawn him to the church: if some hostile opponent of thine who was furiously mad against thee, laying aside his fury, has become gentle, and owned God, and now barks at thee no more, but cries with thee against wickedness: these things are pleasant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what do we rejoice for, if we do not rejoice for these things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or what other are our joys, but these?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because tribulations also abound, and temptations, and dissensions, and schisms, and other evils, without which this world cannot be, until iniquity pass away: let not that rejoicing make us secure, but let our heart be so made glad, as to fear the name of the Lord, lest it be made glad on one side, be stricken on another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Expect not security in journeying: if ever we wish for it here, it will be the birdlime of the body, not the safety of the man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let my heart be made glad, so that it may fear Thy name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will confess unto Thee, O Lord my God, in my whole heart, and I will glorify Thy name for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 12): “for great is Thy mercy toward me, and Thou hast delivered my soul from the nethermost hell” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not be angry, brethren, if I do not explain what I have said as though I were certain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I am a man, and as much as is granted to me concerning the sacred Scriptures, so much I venture to speak: nothing of myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hades I have not yet seen, nor have you: and there will be perhaps another way for us, and not through Hades.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These things are uncertain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because Scripture, which cannot be gainsaid, says, “Thou hast delivered my soul from the nether-most hell,” we understand that there are as it were two hells, an upper one and a lower one: for how can there be a lower hell, unless because there is also an upper?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The one would not be called lower, except by comparison with that upper part.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It appears then, my brethren, that there is some heavenly abode of Angels: there is there a life of ineffable joys, there immortality and incorruption, there all things abiding according to the gift and grace of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That part of the creation is above.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then that is above, but this earthly part, where is flesh and blood, where is corruptibleness, where is nativity and mortality, departure and succession, changeableness and inconstancy, where are fears, desires, horrors, uncertain joys, frail hope, perishable existence; I suppose that all this part cannot be compared with that heaven of which I was just now speaking; if then this part cannot be compared with that, the one is above, the other below.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whither do we go after death, unless there is a depth deeper than this depth in which we are in the flesh and in this mortal state?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “the body is dead,” saith the Apostle, “because of sin.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore even here are the dead; that thou mayest not wonder because it is called infernum, if it abounds with the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he saith not, the body is about to die: but, “the body is dead.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even now surely our body hath life: and yet compared with that body which is to be like the bodies of Angels, the body of man is found to be dead, although still having life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But again, from this infernum, that is from this part of Hades, there is another lower, whither the dead go: from whence God would rescue our souls, even sending thither His own Son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was on account of these two hells, my brethren, that the Son of God was sent, on all sides setting free.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this hell he was sent by being born, to that by dying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore it is His voice in that Psalm, not according to any man’s conjecture, but an Apostle explaining, when he saith, “For Thou wilt not leave my soul in hell.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore it is here also either His voice, “Thou hast delivered my soul from the nethermost hell:” or our voice by the Lord Jesus Christ Himself: for on this account He came even unto hell, that we might not remain in hell.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will mention another opinion also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For perhaps even in hell itself there is some lower part where are thrust the ungodly who have sinned most.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whether in hell there were not some places where Abraham was, we cannot define sufficiently.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not yet had the Lord come to hell that He might rescue from thence the souls of all the saints who had gone before, and yet Abraham was there in repose.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And a certain rich man when he was in torments in hell, when he saw Abraham, lifted up his eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He could not have seen him by lifting up his eyes, unless the one was above, the other below.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what did Abraham answer unto him, when he said, “send Lazarus.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My son,” he said, “remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things, and likewise Lazarus evil things: but now he is at rest, but thou art tormented.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And besides this,” he said, “between us and you there is a great gulf fixed, so that neither can we go to you, nor can any one come from thence to us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore between these two hells, perhaps, in one of which the souls of the just have gotten rest, in the other the souls of the ungodly are tormented, one waiting and praying here, placed here in the body of Christ, and praying in the voice of Christ, said that God had delivered his soul from the nethermost hell, because He delivered him from such sins as might have been the means of drawing him down to the torments of the nethermost hell.…Some one having a troublesome cause was to be sent to prison: another comes and defends him; what does he say when he thanks him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast delivered my soul out of prison.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A debtor was to be hanged up: his debt is paid; he is said to be delivered from being hanged up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were not in all these evils: but because they were in such due course towards them, that unless aid had been brought, they would have been in them, they rightly say that they are delivered from thence, whither they were not suffered by their deliverers to be taken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, whether it be this or that, consider me to be herein an inquirer into the word of God, not a rash assertor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, the transgressors of the law have arisen up against me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom calleth he transgressors of the law?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not the Pagans, who have not received the law: for no one transgresseth that which he hath not received; the Apostle saith clearly, “For where there is no law, there is no prevarication.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Transgressors of the law he calls “prevaricators.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom then do we understand, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we take this word from our Lord Himself, the transgressors of the law were the Jews.…They did not keep the law, and accused Christ as if He transgressed the law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And we know what the Lord suffered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thinkest thou His Body suffers no such thing now?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How can this be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If they called the Master of the house Beelzebub, how much more those of his household?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The disciple is not above his master, nor the servant above his lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The body also suffereth transgressors of the law, and they rise up against the Body of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the transgressors of the law?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do the Jews perchance dare to rise up against Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No: for it is not they that cause us much trouble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they have not yet believed: they have not yet owned their salvation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against the Body of Christ bad Christians rise up, from whom the Body of Christ daily suffereth trouble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All schisms, all heresies, all within who live wickedly and engraft their own character on those who live well, and draw them over to their own side, and with evil communications corrupt good manners; these persons “transgressing the law rose up against Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let every pious soul speak, let every Christian soul speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That one which suffers not this, let it not speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if it is a Christian soul, it knows that it suffers evils: if it owns in itself its own sufferings, let it own herein its own voice; but if it is without suffering, let it also be without the voice; but that it may not be without suffering, let it walk along the narrow way, and begin to live godly in Christ: it must of necessity suffer this persecution.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “all,” saith the Apostle, “who will live godly in Christ, suffer persecution.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the synagogue of the powerful have sought after My soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The synagogue of the powerful is the congregation of the proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The synagogue of the powerful rose up against the Head, that is, our Lord Jesus Christ, crying and saying with one mouth, Crucify Him, crucify Him: of whom it is said, “The sons of men, their teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They did not strike, but cried: by crying they struck, by crying they crucified Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The will of those who cried was fulfilled, when the Lord was crucified: “And they did not place Thee before their eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How did they not place Him before them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They did not know Him God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They should have spared him as Man: what they saw, according to this they should have walked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Suppose that He was not God, He was man: was He therefore to be slain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Spare Him a man, and own Him God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thou, Lord God, art One who hast compassion and merciful, longsuffering, and very pitiful, and true” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore longsuffering and very pitiful, and One who hast compassion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because hanging on the Cross He said: “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom prayeth He to? for whom doth He pray?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who prayeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where prayeth He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Son prays to the Father, crucified for the ungodly, in the midst of very insults, not of words but of death inflicted, hanging on the Cross; as if for this He had His hands stretched out, that thus He might pray for them, that His “prayer might be directed like incense in the sight of the Father, and the lifting up of His hands like an evening sacrifice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore Thou art “true,” “Look upon me, and have mercy upon me: give power unto Thy servant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Thou art “true,” “give power unto Thy servant” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the time of patience pass away, the time of judgment come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How, “give power”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He rising again will come even to earth Himself to judge: He will appear terrible who appeared despicable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will show His power, who showed His patience; on the Cross was patience; in the judgment will be power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He will appear as Man judging, but in glory: because “as ye saw Him go,” said the Angels, “so He will come.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His very form shall come to judgment; therefore the ungodly also shall see Him: for they shall not see the form of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.…In the vision of the Father there is also the vision of the Son: and in the vision of the Son there is also the vision of the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore He adds a consequence, and says: “Know ye not that I am in the Father, and the Father in Me?” that is, both in Me seen the Father is seen, and in the Father seen the Son too is seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The vision of the Father and the Son cannot be separated: where nature and substance is not separated, there vision cannot be separated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that ye may know that the heart ought to be made ready for that place, to see the Divinity of the Father and Son and Holy Spirit, in which though not seen we believe, and by believing cleanse the heart that there may be able to be sight: the Lord Himself saith in another place, “He that hath My commands and keepeth them, he it is that loveth Me: and he that loveth Me shall be loved by My Father: and I will love him, and will manifest Myself unto him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did they not see Him, with whom He was talking?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They both saw Him, and did not see Him? they saw something, they believed something: they saw Man, they believed in God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in the Judgment they shall see the same Lord Jesus Christ as Man, together with the wicked: after the Judgment, they shall see God, apart from the wicked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And save the Son of Thine handmaid.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord is the Son of the handmaid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what handmaid?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Her who when He was announced as about to be born of her, answered and said, “Behold the handmaid of the Lord: be it unto me according to Thy word.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saved the Son of His handmaid, and His own Son: His own Son, in the Form of God; the Son of His handmaid in the form of a servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of the handmaid of God, therefore, the Lord was born in the form of a servant; and He said, “Save the Son of Thine handmaid.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He was saved from death, as ye know, His flesh, which was dead, being raised again.…And each several Christian placed in the Body of Christ may say, “Save the Son of Thine handmaid.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps he cannot say, “Give power unto Thy servant:” because it was He, the Son, who received power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet wherefore saith He not this also?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was it not said to servants, “Ye shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel”? and the servants say, “Know ye not that we shall judge Angels?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Each one therefore of the saints receiveth also power, and each several saint is the son of His handmaid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if he is born of a pagan mother, and has become a Christian?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How can the son of a pagan be the son of His handmaid: He is indeed the son of a pagan mother after the flesh, but the son of the Church after the Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Show me a sign for good” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sign, but that of the Resurrection?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord says: “This wicked and provoking generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given it, but the sign of the Prophet Jonah.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore in our Head a sign has been shown already for good; each one of us also may say, “Show me a sign for good:” because at the last trumpet, at the coming of the Lord, both “the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This will be a sign for good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That they who hate me may see it, and be ashamed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the judgment they shall be ashamed unto their destruction, who will not now be ashamed unto their healing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now therefore let them be ashamed: let them accuse their own ways, let them keep the good way: because none of us liveth without being ashamed, unless he first be ashamed and live anew.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now God grants them the approach of a healthy shame, if they despise not the medicine of confession: but if they will not now be ashamed, then they shall be ashamed, when “their iniquities shall convince them to their face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How shall they be ashamed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they shall say, “These are they whom we had sometimes in derision, and a parable of reproach.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We fools counted their life madness: how are they numbered among the children of God!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath pride profited us?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then shall they say this: let them say it now, and they say it to their health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For let each one turn humbly to God, and now say, What hath my pride profited me? and hear from the Apostle, “For what glory had ye in those things of which ye are now ashamed?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see that there is even now a wholesome shame while there is a place of penitence: but then one which will be late, useless, fruitless.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou, Lord, hast holpen me, and comforted me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hast holpen me,” in struggle; “and comforted me,” in sorrow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no one seeketh comfort, but he who is in misery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would ye not be consoled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say that ye are happy, and ye hear, “My people” (now ye answer, and I hear a murmur, as of persons who remember the Scriptures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May God, who hath written this in your hearts, confirm it in your deeds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see, brethren, that those who say unto you, Ye are happy, seduce you), “O My people, they that call you happy cause you to err, and disturb the way of your feet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also from the Epistle of the Apostle James: “Be afflicted, and mourn: let your laughter be turned to mourning.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see what ye have heard read: when would such things be said unto us in the land of security?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This surely is the land of offences, and temptations, and of all evils, that we may groan here, and deserve to rejoice there; here to be troubled, and there to be comforted, and to say, “For Thou hast delivered mine eyes from tears, my feet from falling: I will please the Lord in the land of the living.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the land of the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The land of the dead passeth, the land of the living cometh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the land of the dead is labour, grief, fear, tribulation, temptation, groaning, sighing: here are false happy ones, true unhappy, because happiness is false, misery is true.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he that owneth himself to be in true misery, will also be in true happiness: and yet now because thou art miserable, hear the Lord saying, “Blessed are they that mourn.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O blessed they that mourn!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing is so akin to misery as mourning: nothing so remote and contrary to misery as blessedness: Thou speakest of those who mourn, and Thou callest them blessed!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand, He saith, what I say: I call those who mourn blessed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore blessed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore mourning?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they mourn in this death, in these tribulations, in their wandering: and because they own themselves to be in this misery, and mourn, they are blessed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore do they mourn?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The blessed Cyprian was put to sorrow in his passion: now he is comforted with his crown; now though comforted, he was sad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For our Lord Jesus Christ still intercedeth for us: all the Martyrs who are with Him intercede for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their intercessions pass not away, except when our mourning is passed away: but when our mourning shall have passed away, we all with one voice, in one people, in one country, shall receive comfort, thousands of thousands joined with Angels playing upon harps, with choirs of heavenly powers living in one city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who mourneth there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who there sigheth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who there toileth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who there needeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who dieth there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who there showeth mercy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who breaketh bread to the hungry there, where all are satisfied with the bread of righteousness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one saith unto thee, Receive a stranger; there no one will be a stranger to thee: all live in their own country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one saith unto thee, Set at one thy friends disputing; in everlasting peace they enjoy the Face of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one saith unto thee, Visit the sick; health and immortality abide for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one saith unto thee, Bury the dead; all shall be in everlasting life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Works of mercy stop, because misery is found not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what shall we do there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall we perhaps sleep?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If now we fight against ourselves, although we carry about a house of sleep, this flesh of ours, and keep watch with these lights, and this solemn feast gives us a mind to watch; what wakefulness shall that day give unto us!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore we shall be awake, we shall not sleep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall we do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There will be no works of mercy, because there will be no misery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps there will be these necessary works which there are here now, of sowing, ploughing, cooking, grinding, weaving?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: None of these, for there will be no want.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus there will be no works of mercy, because misery is past away: where there is no want nor misery, there will be neither works of necessity nor of mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What will be there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What business shall we have?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What action?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will there be no action, because there is rest?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall we sit there, and be torpid, and do nothing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If our love grow cold, our action will grow cold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then will that love resting in the face of God, for whom we now long, for whom we sigh, how will it inflame us, when we shall have come to Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He for whom while as yet we see Him not, we so sigh, how will He enlighten us, when we shall have come to Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How will He change us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What will He make of us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then shall we do, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Psalm tell us: “Blessed are they who dwell in Thy house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall praise Thee for ever and ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This will be our employment, praise of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou lovest and praisest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt cease to praise, if thou cease to love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou wilt not cease to love, because He whom thou seest is such an One as offends thee not by any weariness: He both satisfies thee, and satisfies thee not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What I say is wonderful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If I say that He satisfies thee, I am afraid lest as though satisfied thou shouldest wish to depart, as from a dinner or from a supper.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then do I say? doth He not satisfy thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am afraid again, that if I say, He doth not satisfy thee, thou shouldest seem to be in want: and shouldest be as it were empty, and there should be in thee some void which ought to be filled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then shall I say, except what can be said, but can hardly be thought?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He both satisfies thee, and satisfies thee not: for I find both in Scripture.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For while He said, “Blessed are the hungry, for they shall be filled;” it is again said of Wisdom, “Those who eat Thee shall hunger again, and those who drink shall thirst again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, but He did not say “again,” but he said, “still:” for “shall thirst again” is as if once having been filled he departed and digested, and returned to drink.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So it is, “Those who eat Thee shall still hunger:” thus when they eat they hunger: and those who drink Thee, even thus when drinking, thirst.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it, to thirst in drinking?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Never to grow weary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then there shall be that ineffable and eternal sweetness, what doth He now seek of us, brethren, but faith unfeigned, firm hope, pure charity? and man may walk in the way which the Lord hath given, may bear troubles, and receive consolations.
